jeṭṭhāpacāyino.meaning: paying respect to elders; honouring one's elders
jeṭṭhāpacāyino.summary: This is a compound adjective meaning "one who pays respect to elders".
breakdown: Paying respect, honouring. (The two words coalesce: jeṭṭha + apacāyī → jeṭṭhāpacāyī.)

jeṭṭhāpacāyitā.meaning: respect for elders; the honouring of one's elders

jhāyino.gram: masculine agent noun (jhāyī, from jhāyati "to meditate"; here plural/genitive form)

jighacchā.gram: feminine abstract noun (desiderative formation from √ghas "to eat")
breakdown: Root: to eat. The desiderative reduplication jighacchati means "to wish to eat", hence hunger.
Root: to protect, shun; in the desiderative form jigucchati, "to be disgusted at, to loathe".

jigucchitvā.meaning: having been disgusted; having felt revulsion
breakdown: Stem of jigucchati "to be disgusted, to loathe" (desiderative of √gup).

jinasāsanavarampi.meaning: and the excellent teaching of the Conqueror
jinasāsanavarampi.summary: This is a compound with the enclitic pi attached: "the excellent (true) teaching of the Conqueror, and".
jinasāsanavarampi.gram: compound noun (neuter/masculine accusative) + enclitic particle pi
breakdown: Conqueror — an epithet of the Buddha, who has conquered the defilements.
The teaching, the dispensation of the Buddha.

jino.summary: Here a past participle of jayati "to conquer"; not to be confused with Jina, "the Conqueror", an epithet of the Buddha.
jino.gram: past participle of jayati/jināti (masculine nominative singular)
breakdown: Root: to conquer, to win (jayati, jināti).
Tongue; one of the six sense doors (the base of taste).

jiṇṇakaṁ.gram: adjective (from jiṇṇa, past participle of jīrati "to grow old"; here accusative/neuter singular)
breakdown: Root: to decay, to grow old.

jotiparāyaṇo.meaning: having light as one's goal; destined for light
jotiparāyaṇo.summary: Describes one heading from darkness or light toward light, as in the four types of persons taught in the suttas.
breakdown: Having as final goal, destined for, ending in.

jutimanto.gram: adjective (juti + possessive suffix -mant; here nominative plural)

jutiñca.gram: feminine noun (accusative singular jutiṁ) + enclitic ca, joined by sandhi
breakdown: Splendour, effulgence (accusative of juti, from √jut "to shine").
And. (The niggahīta ṁ + ca becomes ñca by sandhi.)

jālavāgurādihatthe.meaning: with nets, snares and so forth in hand

jāniṁ.gram: feminine noun (jāni, from jahati "to abandon"; accusative singular)
breakdown: Root: to abandon, lose (jahati); weakened to jā- in this formation.

jātarūparajatapaṭiggahaṇā.summary: This compound names one of the things abstained from in the training precepts: the acceptance of gold and silver (money).
jātarūparajatapaṭiggahaṇā.gram: neuter action noun, compound (here ablative: "from accepting gold and silver")

jātassa āpādeta.meaning: one who brings up the one born; a rearer of the child
jātassa āpādeta.gram: phrase: genitive of jāta ("of the one born") + agent noun from āpādeti, causative of āpajjati
breakdown: Of the one born (genitive of jāta, past participle of jāyati "to be born").
One who brings up to a certain state, who rears; agent noun from āpādeti, causative of āpajjati "to enter, fall into, reach".
jātassa āpādeta.note: uncertain: the lexicon gives this as a phrase fragment; the parse follows the lexicon's gloss "birth + one who brings up to a certain state".

jātidhammā.meaning: subject to birth; having the nature of being born
breakdown: Here as final member of a compound: "having the nature of, subject to".

jātipi.summary: As in the First Noble Truth formula: "birth too is suffering" (jātipi dukkhā).
jātipi.gram: feminine noun (jāti, nominative singular) + enclitic particle pi

jātitthaddho.meaning: proud of birth; haughty about one's descent
breakdown: Hard, rigid; haughty, selfish. (The doubling tth is junction doubling.)

jāṇussoṇibrāhmaṇasuttaṁ.meaning: the Discourse to the Brahmin Jāṇussoṇi
jāṇussoṇibrāhmaṇasuttaṁ.gram: proper noun, neuter (title of a sutta)
breakdown: Name of a brahmin who questioned the Buddha.
Brahmin, member of the priestly caste.

jīraṇatā.meaning: decay; the state of growing old

jīvitaṁ.gram: neuter noun (past participle of jīvati used as noun; nominative/accusative singular)
breakdown: Past participle suffix; here the participle serves as a noun, "life".

jīvitindriyassupacchedo.meaning: the cutting off of the life faculty; destruction of the faculty of life
breakdown: Faculty; jīvitindriya is the life faculty.
Genitive: "of the faculty". (The final -a fuses with the following u-.)

jīvitindriyupacchedakaupakkamasamuṭṭhāpi.meaning: occasioning an attack that cuts off the life faculty

breakdown: Addicted to, devoted to, applying oneself to.

jūtappamādassa.meaning: of the negligence of gambling; of heedlessness in dice-play
breakdown: Negligence, heedlessness. (The doubling pp is junction doubling.)

jūtappamādaṭṭhānānuyogo.meaning: addiction to gambling, an occasion for heedlessness
jūtappamādaṭṭhānānuyogo.summary: One of the channels for dissipating wealth taught in the Siṅgālasutta.
breakdown: Being addicted to, applying oneself to.
Suffix: "drinking/dwelling"; the kacchapa is the marsh-dweller, the turtle.

kacci.meaning: truly?; surely?; an interrogative particle expecting confirmation

kadāci.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line for this row is garbled (it runs together with neighbouring rows such as kappati and kampati); the meaning given follows the standard sense of kadāci supported by the DPD analysis.

kahāpaṇā.meaning: kahāpaṇas, the coins of those days
breakdown: A square copper, silver or gold coin; the standard currency unit of ancient India.
Rough, harsh; used of harsh speech.

kalahajātā.meaning: fallen into strife; embroiled in quarrel
breakdown: Produced, born, arisen (past participle of jāyati).

kalahappavaḍḍhanī.meaning: increasing quarrels; that which makes strife grow
breakdown: Increasing, making grow (feminine). (The doubling pp is junction doubling.)

kalyāṇamittassetaṁ.meaning: this is... of one who has virtuous friends
kalyāṇamittassetaṁ.gram: sandhi fusion: genitive adjective + pronoun (kalyāṇamittassa + etaṁ)
breakdown: One who has good, virtuous friends; friendship with the virtuous.
This. (The final -a of -assa elides into e- of etaṁ.)

kalyāṇamittatā.meaning: friendship with the virtuous; having wholesome, beneficial friends
kalyāṇamittatā.summary: Often taught alongside kalyāṇasahāyatā (companionship with the virtuous) and kalyāṇasampavaṅkatā (being colleagues of the virtuous).
kalyāṇamittatā.goenka: Association with the virtuous is given great weight in the suttas studied in this tradition: kalyāṇamittatā, friendship with those established in Dhamma, supports a meditator's own growth in sīla, samādhi and paññā. The teaching groups it with kalyāṇasahāyatā, companionship with the virtuous, and kalyāṇasampavaṅkatā, being colleagues of the virtuous — together describing an environment in which wholesome qualities can develop.

kalyāṇasampavaṅkatā.meaning: being colleagues of the virtuous; close association with the good

kalyāṇādhimuttikā.meaning: inclined to the beneficial; intent upon the wholesome
breakdown: Beneficial, wholesome. (Final -a coalesces with following a-.)

kalāpa.meaning: the smallest indivisible unit of matter as experienced in advanced vipassanā
breakdown: Literally a bundle or sheaf; in Goenka-tradition usage, the sub-atomic particle of matter.
kalāpa.goenka: In Goenkaji's discourses, especially in the advanced portions of the 10-day course, kalāpa names the smallest indivisible unit of matter: as vipassanā deepens, the meditator experiences the entire material structure of the body as a mass of tiny kalāpas arising and passing away with great rapidity. This direct experience of matter as vibration dissolves the illusion of solidity and confirms anicca at the most subtle level.
kalāpa.note: uncertain: this term is not attested in the three-volume Exploring the Path series; the gloss is a placeholder drawn from conventional Goenka-tradition usage (10-day discourses, Days 5 and 8) and requires verification by a human reviewer before being treated as authoritative.

kamati.meaning: to go; to enter into; to come to, get to

kamma.meaning: action; volitional action; the law of action and result
kamma.summary: Kamma is volitional action — mental, verbal or physical — together with the law by which such action bears its corresponding fruit.
breakdown: Noun suffix; kamma is literally "the deed, what is done".
kamma.goenka: In this tradition kamma is understood as volitional action: the performance of kamma is nothing but the determined realisation and purposeful accomplishment of one's positive or negative volition. Kamma is wholesome or unwholesome volition, and one's present condition is the ripened fruit of past volition and the actions derived from it. The emphasis is on complete personal responsibility, captured in the recurring reflection from the Abhiṇhapaccavekkhitabbaṭhānasutta: "Kamma is my only possession, I am heir to my kamma, I am born from my kamma, kamma is my relative, kamma is my reconciliation; whatever kamma I perform, wholesome or unwholesome, I will be the heir thereof." The term should not be flattened into "fate" or the Hindu cosmic-justice sense of "karma" — the weight falls on present volition and its results.

kammabandhu.meaning: having kamma as one's relative; kamma is one's kin
kammabandhu.summary: One term in the reflection "kamma is my relative" from the recollection on kamma.

kammadāyādo.summary: From the same reflection: "I am heir to my kamma".
breakdown: Heir: dāya + ā + da — receiving the son's portion, getting what is left.

kammakiliṭṭhamattano.meaning: one's own impure deed; the stained action of oneself
kammakiliṭṭhamattano.gram: sandhi fusion: neuter noun + adjective + genitive pronoun (kamma + kiliṭṭhaṁ + attano)
breakdown: Impure, stained, defiled (past participle of kilissati).
Of oneself, one's own (genitive of attā).

kammantasaṁvidhānena.meaning: by the arrangement of work; through the proper organisation of one's tasks

breakdown: Suffix extending the stem; kammanta covers action, work and occupation, as in sammā-kammanta, right action.
